skill.name: project-to-obsidian
skill.description:
将代码项目转换为 Obsidian 知识库。当用户提到 obsidian、项目文档、知识库、分析项目、转换项目 时激活。

【激活后必须执行】：
1. 先完整阅读本 SKILL.md 文件
2. 理解 AI 写入规则（默认到 00_Inbox/AI/、追加式、统一 Schema）
3. 执行 STEP 0: 使用 AskUserQuestion 询问用户确认
4. 用户确认后才开始 STEP 1 项目扫描
5. 严格按 STEP 0 → 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 顺序执行

【禁止行为】：
- 禁止不读 SKILL.md 就开始分析项目
- 禁止跳过 STEP 0 用户确认
- 禁止直接在 30_Resources 创建（先到 00_Inbox/AI/）
- 禁止自作主张决定输出位置
skill.summary: 将代码项目转换为 Obsidian 知识库
skill.author: Claude Code

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate documentation generator that converts code projects to Obsidian knowledge bases. It requires user confirmation before execution, scans project files for documentation purposes only, and generates structured markdown templates. No malicious intent or harmful behavior detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Chinese text in metadata descriptions is incorrectly flagged as weak crypto. Text like '将代码项目转换为 Obsidian 知识库' contains Unicode characters that match crypto patterns but is actually valid Chinese language metadata.

dangerous_patterns.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Backtick characters are used for markdown code fences and template placeholders like {{run_commands}}. This is documentation template syntax, not actual shell execution code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden File Access (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references .env files in project scanning patterns to detect configuration files. This is legitimate project analysis - the skill does NOT actually read or exfiltrate sensitive environment files.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill scans project directories as intended - this is its core functionality of analyzing project structure and generating documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: High Entropy/JSFuck Obfuscation (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Template placeholders like {{placeholder}} create bracket chains that the scanner misidentifies as obfuscation. These are legitimate documentation template variables.

content.user_title: Convert Code to Obsidian Knowledge Base
content.value_statement: Transform any code project into a searchable, linkable Obsidian knowledge base. The skill analyzes project structure, code, and APIs to automatically generate structured documentation.
actual_capabilities: Analyze project structure and identify project type
Extract functions, classes, and API endpoints from code
Create bidirectional links and tags for knowledge graph
Support multiple programming languages and frameworks
limitations: Requires MCP integration for direct Obsidian vault writing
May not handle very large monorepos efficiently
Limited to supported languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, Ruby, PHP
Output quality depends on code comments and structure

use_cases.title: Developer Documentation
use_cases.description: Automatically generate comprehensive documentation for your codebase including API references, module descriptions, and architecture diagrams.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers who need project documentation

use_cases.title: Knowledge Management
use_cases.description: Convert legacy projects into searchable knowledge bases that can be linked with other notes and explored via bidirectional links.
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads and architects managing multiple projects

use_cases.title: Onboarding Support
use_cases.description: Create structured documentation to help new team members understand project structure, APIs, and setup procedures.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Project Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project-to-obsidian skill to generate a quick overview of my project at [PATH]. Give me the project structure and main components.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick understanding of an unfamiliar codebase

prompt_templates.title: Full Documentation Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /p2o [PATH] to convert my project to an Obsidian knowledge base. Generate complete documentation including architecture, API docs, and module descriptions.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need comprehensive project documentation

prompt_templates.title: API Documentation Only
prompt_templates.prompt: Use project-to-obsidian to generate API documentation for [PATH]. Focus on endpoints, request/response formats, and function signatures.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to document APIs for external consumers

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /p2o [PATH] --arch to analyze and document the architecture of my project. Include system design, dependencies, and component relationships.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to understand or document system architecture

output: Project Overview created with structure, dependencies, and language detection
API Documentation section with endpoints and request/response schemas
Quickstart guide with installation and usage instructions

output_examples.input: 把这个React项目转成obsidian知识库
output: Created component documentation with props and state
Added integration points and hooks usage

best_practices: Always confirm output location with user before writing files
Use the recommended 00_Inbox/AI/ directory for initial output
Run with user confirmation step enabled to prevent accidental writes
Choose the appropriate generation mode (full, quick, API, or architecture)
anti_patterns: Do not skip the user confirmation step (STEP 0)
Do not write directly to 30_Resources without going through Inbox first
Do not analyze projects without reading the SKILL.md first
Do not overwrite existing notes - use append mode

faq.question: What programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, Ruby, and PHP. It detects project type via config files like package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, etc.

faq.question: How does it write to Obsidian?
faq.answer: The skill can write directly to Obsidian vault using MCP tools (obsidian_append_content), create local folders, or output to project /docs directory.

faq.question: Does it read my .env files?
faq.answer: The skill scans for .env* patterns to detect configuration files but does NOT read or exfiltrate sensitive environment variables.

faq.question: Can I customize the output structure?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses customizable templates for different document types. You can modify the templates in the templates/ directory.

faq.question: What if my project is very large?
faq.answer: For large projects, use the quick mode (--quick) to generate just the overview and structure first, then generate specific sections as needed.

faq.question: Is this safe to use with production code?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill only reads and analyzes your code locally. It does not make network requests or execute arbitrary commands. User confirmation is required before any file writing.
